workflow.name: Analyze vendor security risk from Jira tickets using Grok, Claude and Slack
workflow.description:
## How it works

When a new vendor ticket is created in Jira, this workflow automatically performs a comprehensive security due-diligence investigation and posts the findings back as a Jira comment plus a Slack notification.

1. A Jira webhook fires when an issue is created. An AI agent (Grok) extracts the vendor company name, URL, and product from the issue fields.
2. A Seed Discovery agent (Claude + Perplexity) finds the vendor's official domains, trust center, privacy policy, and status page URLs — handling domain aliases automatically.
3. Seven specialized research agents run **in parallel**, each with a focused persona: Compliance, Data Handling, Privacy & Legal, Security Controls, Availability, Pricing, and Company Intel. All use Perplexity sonar-pro-search for live web research.
4. A Sanitizer code node strips unpredictable LLM formatting from all 7 outputs.
5. A Lead Security Analyst agent (Grok) synthesizes all findings into a risk-scored report, generating Jira Wiki Markup and Slack Block Kit JSON simultaneously.
6. The report is posted as a comment on the original Jira ticket, and a rich Slack notification with a "View Report in Jira" button is sent to your security channel.

## Set up steps

- **OpenRouter** — Create an OpenRouter account and add an API credential in n8n. The workflow uses three models: `x-ai/grok-4-fast`, `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.5`, and `perplexity/sonar-pro-search` (~10 min)
- **Jira** — Add a Jira credential (API token) and configure a Jira webhook automation to POST to this workflow's webhook URL when an issue is created in your vendor-review project (~10 min)
- **Slack** — Add a Slack credential (OAuth) and set your target channel in the "Send a message" node (~5 min)
- **Jira URL** — In the "AI Agent" node, replace `YOUR-JIRA-DOMAIN` in the Jira Browser URL with your actual Atlassian domain (~1 min)

assignments.name: output.effectiveQuery
output.companyUrl_norm
assignments.value: ={{   (() => {     const u = ($json.output.companyUrl ?? '').toString().trim();     if (!u) return null;     if (/^https?:\/\//i.test(u)) return u;     return 'https://' + u;   })() }}

assignments.name: output.domain

parameters.text:
=You are a precise entity extractor for SAAS due-diligence automation. 
Analyze the incoming JSON payload from Jira webhook and extract exactly three fields: companyName, companyUrl, productName.
{{ JSON.stringify($json) }}

Priority rules (follow strictly in order):
1. Find the vendor URL first — this is the primary source:
   - Prefer explicit URLs in any field: customfield_*, description, summary, changelog, or body.
   - Especially look for fields like customfield_12270 (often "Vendor URL" or "Website").
   - If URL found (e.g., "https://www.clay.com/" or "clay.com"), use it as companyUrl.
   - Normalize: add https:// if missing, strip path/query → only origin (e.g., https://www.clay.com/ → https://www.clay.com).

2. companyName:
   - Derive from companyUrl root domain: 
     - clay.com → "Clay"
     - upstash.com → "Upstash"
     - chatgpt.com → "ChatGPT"
     - acme.com → "Acme"
   - Remove "www.", capitalize properly.
   - If explicit company name exists in description/summary and differs from domain-derived, prefer explicit only if it's clearly the vendor (e.g., "OpenAI" for chatgpt.com).
   - Do NOT use issue summary as companyName unless no URL found.

3. productName:
   - Prefer explicit product mention near URL or in summary/description.
   - If summary = "Clay" and URL = "https://www.clay.com/", then productName = "Clay" (same as company).
   - If summary = "ChatGPT Atlas" and URL = "https://chatgpt.com/atlas/", then productName = "ChatGPT Atlas".
   - If path in URL suggests product (e.g., /atlas, /redis), use it only if no better name.
   - If no distinction, productName can equal companyName.

4. Fallbacks:
   - If no URL → try headers.host (e.g., your-instance.app.n8n.cloud → ignore, not vendor).
   - If still no URL → companyUrl = null.
   - If no clear name → companyName = null.

5. Output ONLY valid JSON — no text, no explanations:
{
  "companyName": string | null,
  "companyUrl": string | null,
  "productName": string | null,
  "source": {
    "companyName": "brief description or JSON path",
    "companyUrl": "brief description or JSON path",
    "productName": "brief description or JSON path"
  }
}

Examples:
- URL: "https://www.clay.com/", summary: "Clay" → 
  companyName: "Clay", companyUrl: "https://www.clay.com", productName: "Clay", source: {companyUrl: "customfield_12270", ...}

- URL: "https://chatgpt.com/atlas/", summary: "ChatGPT Atlas" → 
  companyName: "ChatGPT", companyUrl: "https://chatgpt.com", productName: "ChatGPT Atlas"

Now analyze the provided JSON payload and return the JSON object.

options.systemMessage:
You are the Orchestrator agent for an evidence-driven security research pipeline. 
Your role: Accept input payload {requestId, companyName, url, depth, max_sources, timeout_seconds, hints (optional), cache_key (optional), productName (optional)}. 

Normalize inputs:
  • Add https:// to url if missing.
  • Generate cache_key = sha256(companyName + url + (productName or "")) if not provided.
  • Compute effectiveQuery = productName ? "{{companyName}} {{productName}}" : "{{companyName}}".

Check cache for fresh results (TTL=7 days; if cached and valid, return it immediately).

Dispatch 7 Worker agents in parallel (compliance, data-handling, privacy, security-controls, availability, pricing, company-intel), each with:
  • Full payload
  • effectiveQuery
  • Section-specific instructions

Aggregate responses while preserving all evidence:
  • Use Chain-of-Thought to detect contradictions (company vs. product vs. third-party).
  • Compute confidence per rules.
  • Derive data_categories and data_sensitivity (product-aware mapping).
  • Flag missing keys in missing_info.

Enforce timeouts by monitoring duration_ms; retry once on transient errors.
Persist evidence snapshots locally or via cache.
Prioritize official sources (domain matches url) — if productName set, prioritize product-specific pages (e.g., plugin docs, GitHub, API portal).

Use deterministic behavior (temperature=0.0).
Output ONLY the final JSON matching the schema — no free text, explanations, or extra keys.
If aggregation fails, include error in status field.

parameters.text:
=Analyze this vendor:
- Company Name: {{ $json.output.companyName }}
- Input URL: {{ $json.output.companyUrl_norm }}
- Product Name: {{ $json.output.productName }}

INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Call `perplexity_sub_agent` tool with query: "[Company Name] official website trust center security compliance".
2. Call `perplexity_sub_agent` tool with query: "site:[Input Domain] OR site:[Company Name] privacy policy terms dpa".
3. ANALYZE IDENTITY:
   - Does the search result redirect to a different domain? (e.g., input `manus.im` -> result `manus.ai`)?
   - If yes, treat the redirect target as the `primary_domain`.
4. EXTRACT URLS:
   - Map found URLs to the output schema.
   - For 'trust_portal', look for subdomains like `trust.`, `security.`, or paths like `/security`, `/compliance`.

options.systemMessage:
=ROLE: Seed Discovery & Identity Verification Agent

GOAL:
You are the first step in a security due-diligence pipeline. Your job is to:
1. Identify the "Official Truth" domains for the company.
2. Handle Domain Aliases (e.g., if input is `manus.im` but real site is `manus.ai`, accept the primary).
3. Return a structured map of URLs for downstream workers.

TOOL USAGE (MANDATORY):
- You are an OFFLINE model. You MUST use `perplexity_sub_agent` to verify links.
- Do NOT guess URLs. Only return URLs found via the tool.

OUTPUT RULES (CRITICAL):
1. **RAW JSON ONLY:** Do NOT use Markdown code blocks (no ```json).
2. **NO CHATTER:** Do NOT add an "Analysis Summary", "Note", or any text before/after the JSON.
3. Start output with `{` and end with `}`.

OUTPUT SCHEMA:
{
  "status": "ok",
  "identity": {
    "input_domain": "...",
    "primary_domain": "...",
    "is_alias": boolean
  },
  "seed_urls": {
    "trust_portal": "url or null",
    "security_page": "url or null",
    "privacy_policy": "url or null",
    "terms": "url or null",
    "subprocessors": "url or null",
    "status_page": "url or null"
  }
}

parameters.text:
=TARGET: {{ $('Add required fields').item.json.output.companyName }}
PRIMARY DOMAIN: {{ $json.output.identity.primary_domain }}
SEED URLS: {{ JSON.stringify($json.output.seed_urls) }}

TASK:
Using the seed URLs (specifically 'trust_portal' and 'security_page') and the `perplexity_sub_agent`:
1. Find evidence of SOC 2 (Type I or II), ISO 27001, PCI DSS, FedRAMP or any other.
2. Check the dates. Are the certs current?
3. If no direct PDF is found, look for a "Request Access" page (common for SOC2).

TARGET: {{ $('Add required fields').item.json.output.companyName }}
PRIMARY DOMAIN: {{ $json.output.identity.primary_domain }}
SEED URLS: {{ JSON.stringify($json.output.seed_urls) }}

TASK:
1. **TRUST CENTER DEEP DIVE (Priority 1):**
   - If a 'trust_portal' URL is provided (e.g., trust.manus.im), you MUST analyze it specifically.
   - Trust Centers often host "Subprocessors", "Security Controls", and "Report Request" buttons.
   - Search specifically for: `site:{{ $json.output.seed_urls.trust_portal }} "SOC 2" OR "ISO 27001" OR "controls" OR "subprocessors"`
   - If the main trust page seems empty, look for sub-pages like `/compliance`, `/security`, `/legal`.

2. **CERTIFICATION VERIFICATION:**
   - Find evidence of SOC 2 (Type I or II), ISO 27001, PCI DSS, or FedRAMP.
   - **Crucial:** Distinguish between "We are SOC 2 Compliant" (marketing claim) vs "Download SOC 2 Report" (evidence).
   - Check dates: Evidence older than 18 months is "Outdated".

3. **GAPS:**
   - If you found the Trust Center but cannot see the *content* of the reports (e.g., locked behind login), Report "Status: Gated/Requestable" rather than "Missing".

options.systemMessage:
ROLE: Compliance Research Specialist
GOAL: Verify formal security attestations (SOC 2, ISO, PCI, FedRAMP).

## TOOL USAGE (MANDATORY)
- You are an OFFLINE model. You MUST use `perplexity_sub_agent`.
- You MUST perform a specific "Deep Dive" search if a Trust Portal URL is provided.

## OUTPUT RULES
1. **RAW JSON ONLY:** No Markdown, no chatter.
2. **STATUS DEFINITIONS:**
   - "Present": Found direct evidence (PDF, badge, text).
   - "Gated": Found a "Request Report" or "NDA" page.
   - "Missing": No evidence found after searching.
3. **EVIDENCE:** Include exact URLs and concise snippets.

OUTPUT SCHEMA:
{
  "agent": "Compliance Agent",
  "key_findings": [
    {
      "topic": "SOC 2|ISO 27001|PCI DSS|FedRAMP|HIPAA",
      "status": "Present|Missing|Gated|Outdated",
      "summary": "e.g. 'SOC 2 Type II report covering 2024-2025 available via Trust Portal.'",
      "url": "..."
    }
  ],
  "evidence": [ { "url": "...", "snippet": "...", "source_type": "official|third_party" } ]
}

parameters.text:
=TARGET: {{ $('Add required fields').item.json.output.companyName }}
PRIMARY DOMAIN: {{ $json.output.identity.primary_domain }}
SEED URLS: {{ JSON.stringify($json.output.seed_urls) }}

TASK:
1. **SUBPROCESSORS:**
   - Search: `site:{{ $json.output.identity.primary_domain }} "subprocessors" OR "sub-processors" OR "third party vendors"`
   - Summary: Do they have a public list?

2. **DPA & RESIDENCY:**
   - Search: `site:{{ $json.output.identity.primary_domain }} "Data Processing Agreement" OR "DPA" OR "EU hosting" OR "data residency"`
   - Summary: Can customers choose EU vs US storage? Is there a standard DPA?

3. **RETENTION:**
   - Search: `site:{{ $json.output.identity.primary_domain }} "data retention" OR "deletion policy" OR "termination"`
   - Summary: What happens to data after the contract ends? (e.g., deleted in 30 days).

options.systemMessage:
ROLE: Data Governance Specialist
GOAL: Map data processing, residency, subprocessors, and retention policies.

## TOOL USAGE (MANDATORY)
- You are an OFFLINE model. Use `perplexity_sub_agent`.

## OUTPUT RULES
1. **RAW JSON ONLY:** No Markdown.
2. **PRIORITY:** Focus on finding the actual DPA PDF or page.

OUTPUT SCHEMA:
{
  "agent": "Data Handling Agent",
  "key_findings": [
    {
      "topic": "DPA|Subprocessors|Data Residency|Retention",
      "status": "Present|Missing|Unclear",
      "summary": "...",
      "url": "..."
    }
  ],
  "evidence": [ { "url": "...", "snippet": "...", "source_type": "official|third_party" } ]
}

ROLE: Privacy Legal Analyst
GOAL: Assess alignment with GDPR, CCPA, and International Data Transfers.

## TOOL USAGE (MANDATORY)
- You are an OFFLINE model. Use `perplexity_sub_agent`.

## OUTPUT RULES
1. **RAW JSON ONLY:** No Markdown.
2. **SPECIFICITY:** Do not just say "They comply with laws". Quote the specific frameworks (GDPR, CCPA, DPF).

OUTPUT SCHEMA:
{
  "agent": "Privacy Agent",
  "key_findings": [
    {
      "topic": "GDPR|CCPA|Transfers|Rights",
      "status": "Explicit|Implied|Missing",
      "summary": "...",
      "url": "..."
    }
  ],
  "evidence": [ { "url": "...", "snippet": "...", "source_type": "official|third_party" } ]
}

parameters.text:
=TARGET: {{ $('Add required fields').item.json.output.companyName }}
PRIMARY DOMAIN: {{ $json.output.identity.primary_domain }}
SEED URLS: {{ JSON.stringify($json.output.seed_urls) }}

TASK:
1. **AUTHENTICATION:**
   - Search: `site:{{ $json.output.identity.primary_domain }} "SSO" OR "SAML" OR "SCIM" OR "Single Sign On"`
   - Search: `site:{{ $json.output.identity.primary_domain }} "MFA" OR "2FA" OR "Multi-factor"`

2. **RED TEAM / INCIDENT HISTORY (CRITICAL):**
   - Search: `"{{ $('Process values').item.json.output.companyName }}" data breach OR security incident OR ransomware OR hacked`
   - Search: `"{{ $('Process values').item.json.output.companyName }}" CVE vulnerabilities exploit`
   - Search: `site:reddit.com "{{ $('Process values').item.json.output.companyName }}" security concern`

3. **VULNERABILITY DISCLOSURE:**
   - Search: `site:{{ $json.output.identity.primary_domain }} "security.txt" OR "bug bounty" OR "responsible disclosure"`
   - Search: `"{{ $('Process values').item.json.output.companyName }}" bug bounty program`

options.systemMessage:
ROLE: Technical Security Analyst
GOAL: Validate existence of SSO, MFA, RBAC, and Vulnerability Disclosure.

## TOOL USAGE (MANDATORY)
- You are an OFFLINE model. Use `perplexity_sub_agent`.

## OUTPUT RULES
1. **RAW JSON ONLY:** No Markdown.
2. **FOCUS:** Differentiate between "Internal security" (what they do) and "Customer features" (SSO/MFA for users). Focus on Customer Features.

OUTPUT SCHEMA:
{
  "agent": "Security Controls Agent",
  "key_findings": [
    {
      "topic": "SSO|MFA|RBAC|VDP",
      "status": "Supported|Not Found|Add-on",
      "summary": "e.g. 'SAML SSO supported via Okta/OneLogin'",
      "url": "..."
    }
  ],
  "evidence": [ { "url": "...", "snippet": "...", "source_type": "official|third_party" } ]
}

parameters.text:
=TARGET: {{ $('Add required fields').item.json.output.companyName }}
PRIMARY DOMAIN: {{ $json.output.identity.primary_domain }}
SEED URLS: {{ JSON.stringify($json.output.seed_urls) }}

TASK:
1. **STATUS PAGE:**
   - If a status URL exists, check it. Does it show historical uptime?
   - If not, search: `site:{{ $json.output.identity.primary_domain }} "status" OR "uptime"`

2. **SLA & DR:**

   - Search: `site:{{ $json.output.identity.primary_domain }}"disaster recovery" OR "RTO" OR "RPO"`

TASK:

options.systemMessage:
ROLE: Procurement Analyst & JSON API
GOAL: Extract commercial terms, pricing tiers, and "SSO Tax" risks.

## TOOL USAGE (MANDATORY)
- You are an OFFLINE model. Use `perplexity_sub_agent`.

## OUTPUT RULES (CRITICAL)
1. **YOU ARE AN API.** Do not speak plain English. Do not write summaries.
2. **RAW JSON ONLY.** No Markdown fences. Start with `{` and end with `}`.
3. If SSO costs extra (e.g., "Enterprise only" or "Flat fee"), mark "SSO Tax" as "Verified".

OUTPUT SCHEMA:
{
  "agent": "Pricing Agent",
  "key_findings": [
    {
      "topic": "Pricing Structure|SSO Tax|Refunds",
      "status": "Verified|Unclear",
      "summary": "Brief factual sentence.",
      "url": "..."
    }
  ],
  "evidence": [ { "url": "...", "snippet": "...", "source_type": "official|third_party" } ]
}

GOAL: Verify Legal Entity, HQ, Funding, and Acquisition status.

## TOOL USAGE (MANDATORY)

## OUTPUT RULES
1. **RAW JSON ONLY:** No Markdown.

OUTPUT SCHEMA:
{

ROLE: Lead Security Analyst (Aggregator)
GOAL: Synthesize 7 research streams into a unified Vendor Security Report for Jira and Slack.

INPUT DATA:
- Research results from 7 agents.
- Jira Key and URL.

INSTRUCTIONS:
1. **Consolidate Evidence:**
   - Merge duplicate URLs.
   - Assign global IDs (E1, E2...).
   - Reference IDs in findings.

2. **Assess Risk:**
   - HIGH: Missing SOC2/ISO, No DPA, No SSO, HIPAA violations.
   - MED: No Status Page, Missing Subprocessor list.

3. **Generate Output JSON:**
   - `jira_body`: Jira Wiki Markup (h1, h2, tables).
   - `slack_blocks`: Native JSON array.
     - Block 1: Header ("Vendor Security Report: <Vendor>")
     - Block 2: Section (Context/Overview)
     - Block 3: Section (Highlights - Top 3 Pros)
     - Block 4: Section (Risks - Top 3 Gaps)
     - Block 5: Actions (Button: "View Report in Jira", URL: <Jira Browser URL>)

OUTPUT JSON SCHEMA:
{
  "jira_body": "string (wiki markup)",
  "slack_blocks": [
    { "type": "header", "...":"..." },
    { "type": "section", "...":"..." },
    { "type": "actions", "elements": [ { "type": "button", "text": {"...":"..."}, "url": "..." } ] }
  ],
  "slack_text": "Short summary for notification"
}

parameters.text:
=Research the following query passed by the lead agent:
"{{ $fromAI('query') }}"

CONSTRAINTS:
1. Max Results: {{ $fromAI('max_sources') || 6 }}
2. Format: Return STRICT JSON only.
3. Content: Focus on extracting facts, dates, and snippets.

options.systemMessage:
=ROLE: Web Search & Extraction Sub-Agent

GOAL:
You are an interface to the real-time web. You receive a specific `query` from a Lead Researcher and must return factual results in JSON format.

INPUT HANDLING:
- You will receive a query variable. You MUST run a live web search for it.
- If the query contains `site:`, strictly filter results to that domain.

OUTPUT RULES (STRICT JSON):
1. Do NOT chat. Do NOT use Markdown (```json). Just return the raw JSON object.
2. Structure:
{
  "results": [
    {
      "title": "Page Title",
      "url": "Full URL",
      "date": "YYYY-MM-DD" or null,
      "snippet": "The specific text relevant to the query.",
      "source_type": "first_party (if matches site: operator) | third_party (reviews/news)"
    }
  ]
}

3. SNIPPET QUALITY:
   - If the query asks for "SOC 2", the snippet MUST contain the text regarding SOC 2.
   - Do not return generic homepage descriptions.

ERROR HANDLING:
- If no results found, return { "results": [] }.

parameters.content:
## 1. Ingestion Pipeline

Receive the Jira webhook and normalize the vendor target (company, URL, product) into a clean payload for downstream agents.

## 2. Discovery Engine

Resolve the vendor's canonical URLs (Trust Center, Privacy Policy, Subprocessors, Status page) before deep research. Prevents downstream agents from hallucinating URLs.

## 3. Parallel Research Swarm

Seven specialist agents run concurrently — Compliance, Data Handling, Privacy & Legal, Security Controls, Availability, Pricing, Company Intel — each with a focused JSON output schema. All share one `perplexity_sub_agent` tool for live web search and the same Claude Sonnet 4.5 brain.

## 4. Synthesis Pipeline

Collapse the 7 asynchronous JSON streams into one clean evidence set: merge → sanitize (strip markdown fences, handle bad JSON) → unwrap → aggregate all `key_findings[]` and `evidence[]` arrays for the Lead Analyst.

## 5. Reporting Layer

Lead Analyst deduplicates evidence, scores risk (High / Med / Low), and produces both Jira Wiki Markup and Slack Block Kit outputs — posted to the original ticket and to your security channel.

## 🔐 AI-Powered Vendor Security Risk Analyzer

### Who's it for
Security teams, procurement, and compliance leads running third-party SaaS vendor risk assessments. If you currently spend an hour per vendor digging through Trust Centers, privacy pages, and sub-processor lists, this workflow condenses that work into ~90 seconds of automated, evidence-linked research.

### What it does
When a security-review ticket is opened in Jira, seven specialist research agents run in parallel across the vendor's official sources. A Lead Analyst scores the risk (High / Med / Low) and posts a consolidated report back to the Jira ticket and to a Slack channel — with direct links to every piece of evidence.

### How it works
1. **Ingest** — Jira webhook fires on ticket creation; an extractor agent parses the payload to pull the vendor's name, URL, and product.
2. **Discover** — A Seed URL Discovery Agent resolves the canonical Trust Center, Privacy Policy, Subprocessors page and Status page. Handles domain aliases (e.g. `manus.im` → `manus.ai`).
3. **Research (fan-out)** — Seven specialist agents run concurrently: Compliance, Data Handling, Privacy & Legal, Security Controls, Availability, Pricing, Company Intel. All share one Perplexity sub-agent for live web search.
4. **Synthesize (fan-in)** — Merge → sanitize JSON → deduplicate evidence → aggregate into a single payload for the Lead Analyst.
5. **Report** — Lead Analyst scores risk, formats Jira Wiki Markup + Slack Block Kit, and posts to both channels.

### How to use
1. Add three credentials: **OpenRouter** (API key), **Jira** (API token with `issueComment` permission), **Slack** (OAuth2 with `chat:write`).
2. Wire your Jira automation rule to POST to this workflow's webhook URL.
3. Replace every ⚠️ placeholder called out in the green setup notes next to each relevant node (Slack `channelId`, Jira domain, vendor-URL custom field ID).
4. Activate the workflow and open a test ticket.

Typical run: ~60–120s end-to-end, ~$0.03–$0.08 per vendor (OpenRouter usage).

### ⚠️ Replace `customfield_12270`
This prompt references our Jira "Vendor URL" field ID. **Change it** to match the custom-field ID used in your Jira instance, otherwise the agent won't find the URL.

### ⚠️ Replace `YOUR-JIRA-DOMAIN`
The agent's prompt builds a Jira link using `YOUR-JIRA-DOMAIN.atlassian.net`. **Replace it** with your Atlassian domain (e.g. `acme.atlassian.net`), otherwise the "View Report" button in Slack will 404.

### ⚠️ Set Slack `channelId`
The Channel ID is empty by default. **Set it** to your security channel's ID, otherwise the Slack post will fail silently.

user.name: Dmitry Uchakin
user.bio: Security Engineer and automation builder. I design n8n workflows that combine AI agents with security tooling — vendor risk, Jira, Slack, threat intel. Clone my templates or reach out for custom builds.
